title: limodorum filiforme
intro: Limodorum filiforme, also known as the adder's tongue orchid or Ophrys filiformis, is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the orchid family. It is a perennial plant with long, thin leaves and a unique appearance, often found in woodland areas and rocky slopes. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, including treating fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, due to its high content of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The plant can be prepared in various herbal forms, such as infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are said to offer several health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects.
